steps.title: Identify the boundary line from the graph
steps.content: The line passes through the origin (0,0) and extends to the lower right. At x = 4, the line reaches y = -4, giving slope = -4/4 = -1. The boundary line equation is $y = -x$.

steps.title: Determine the shaded region inequality
steps.content: The shaded region extends above the boundary line $y = -x$, representing all points where $y > -x$ (equivalently, $x + y > 0$).

steps.title: Find the solution point
steps.content: Looking at the four choices, only (4, 0) lies in the shaded region. Backsolving — testing choice D (4, 0): Does it satisfy $y > -x$? Check: is $0 > -4$? Yes. That matches choice D — D is correct.
